def: n. f. 1. Succession. 2. Row; train.
s.m. Mounting upon; bestriding; leaping or stepping over or across, &c. (see next)
subs: (-ke), To come under the stride (of), to be ridden or mounted (by); to be crossed or passed over (by).

def: s.m. Leaping, jumping; springing, going by leaps or jumps; — leaping or jumping over or across, passing or crossing over, stepping across; exceeding, going beyond bounds, transgressing; transgressing propriety; — springing upon, mounting, bestriding; coition; — fasting, abstinence; fast; starvation; — catching (a disorder) by stepping over (infectious matter, — e.g. catching syphilis by stepping over the urine of one suffering from that disorder — which is popularly believed to be a fact)
more: Catching syphilis by stepping over the urine of a syphilitic patient. (Popular belief).
s. m. 1. Fast, fasting. 2. To jump over. لنگهن پڙنا langhan paṛnā, To catch (as, a disorder).
subs: (-ko), To cross or pass over, &c. ( = laṅghnā); — to go without food, to fast.

def: n. m. S. लघ to fast. Fast; fasting. Pī kāran pīrī bhaī, log kaheṅ pinḍ rog, Chhip chhip laṅghan maiṅ kare, piyā milan ke jog . Dohā. All for my love I'm pale, folks say I'm jaundiced grown, In secret fasts I've kept that I might meet my own. laṅghan k. , v. n. To go without food; keep a fast. Yā hansā motī chuge, yā langhan kar jāë . Prov. The swan or feeds on pearls, or hungry keeps.

ety: caus. of laṅghnā; — laṅghā˚ for langhāw˚ = Prk. लंघाव(इ) or लंघावे(इ) = S. लङ्घ+आपय(ति)
def: v.t. To cause, or make, or help to jump over, &c.
s.f. Fasting, fast, abstinence ( = laṅghan).
part. Leapt over, passed over or beyond, overstepped, transgressed.

ety: Prk. लंघणअं; S. लङ्घनीयं, rt. लङ्घ्
def: v.t. To mount upon, to ride; to sit astride of, to bestride; to jump or spring over, to leap or step over; to overstep, to go beyond, to pass over
more: v. a. S. लङ्घन See ulāṅgnā . Yeh ghoṛī abhī lāṅghī nahīṅ gaī . This mare has not been ridden yet. lāṅgh'an , Crossing over any thing with one's legs. Lāṅghan meṅ āī huī chīz ko nahīṅ khāte . (A superstition). One does not eat what one's legs have passed over.
v. a. To overleap, to pass over.

ety: Prk. लंघणअं = S. लङ्घनीयं, rt. लङ्घ्
def: v.t. To jump over, to leap over or across; to step over or across, to pass or cross over; to stride across, to bestride, to mount; — v.n. To be jumped over; to be passed over; to pass (as time, syn. bītnā).
more: 1. v. n. To be jumped over, to be passed over, to pass. 2. v. a. To jump or leap over.

def: s.f. Urine
more: Tir. n. f. S. लिङ्ग organ of generation. Urine. laṅgī k. , v. n. To discharge urine.

def: n. m. ( Horse dealers ) One hundred rupees. lāṅg mālī rekhe , n. m. ( Horse dealers ) Seven hundred rupees.
v. n. (dakh.) To limp ( Harris ).

s.m. A waist-cloth; a cloth worn between the legs to conceal the privites (a strip of cloth about two feet long and six or eight inches broad, passed between the legs, and the ends tucked into a waist-band before and behind)
subs: To tie (or put) on a laṅgoṭ; to gird up one's loins (for, -par)
s.m. A bandage to which the laṅgoṭ is fastened; — one devoted to celibacy (lit. 'one who keeps his laṅgoṭ fastened'), a confirmed bachelor
s.m. An ascetic (lit. 'true to his laṅgoṭ')
To pull tight and fasten the laṅgoṭ
To open (or unfasten) the laṅgoṭ; — to have sexual intercourse (with)
s.m. (f. -ī) A monkey; an epithet of Hanumān.

def: s. m. See لنگوٿي langoṭī. لنگوٿ بند langoṭ-band, 1. A bandage to which the langoṭ is fastened. 2. One devoted to celibacy.

n. m. Dim. laṅgoṭī , n. f. See tah-band , under tah. Bādshāh kī laṅgoṭī khul gaī . (Chess). The king is checkmated. laṅgoṭ bāṅdhnā , 1. To gird one's loins. 2. To strip for work or fight. laṅgoṭ-band , n. m. A confirmed batchelor; a jatī. laṅgoṭ-phal , Slang, n. m. Membrum virile. laṅgoṭ kā sachchā , v. n. An ascetic. laṅgoṭ kholnā , Slang, v. a. To unbutton one's breeches; hence, to have sexual intercourse with. lāl laṅgoṭ-wālā , n. m. 1. A monkey. 2. An epithet of Hanumān. laṅgoṭī baṅdhvānā , v. a. To leave a strip for the loins. (When highwayman stripped a man they generously left him a strip to hide the privities). laṅgoṭī khulnā , To come down (the front wall of the women's apartments). laṅgoṭī meṅ phāg khelnā , v. n. lit . to play Holī in a langoṭī . To riot and revel in spite of poverty. laṅgoṭiyā yār, laṅgoṭiyā , n. m. 1. A playmate. Sab miliyo, par laṅgoṭiyā na miliyo! Prov. Meet freely all, but playmates of your boyhood e'er keep far. (They know you too well). 2. An old bosom friend. Nahīṅ jis ādmī ke pās izār, Usko samjhe hai yeh laṅgoṭiyā yār . A satire. Saudā.
s.m. = H لنگوٿي लंगोटी laṅgoṭī [Prk. लिंगवट्टआ = S. लिङ्ग+पट्टका], s.f. = laṅgoṭ, q.v.
more: s. m. See لنگوٿي langoṭī. لنگوٿ بند langoṭ-band, 1. A bandage to which the langoṭ is fastened. 2. One devoted to celibacy.

def: s.f. = H لنگوٿا लंगोटा laṅgoṭā [Prk. लिंगवट्टओ = S. लिङ्ग+पट्टकः], s.m. = laṅgoṭ, q.v.
more: s. f. A cloth worn between the legs to conceal the privities. (A bit of cloth about two feet long and six or eight inches broad, passed between the legs, and the ends tucked in before and behind to the tasma or dūʼālī. Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: adj. & s.m. Of or relating to a laṅgoṭī; — a playmate; an old friend (from childhood); a very intimate friend, a crony; — laṅgoṭiyā-yār, s.m. = laṅgoṭiyā.
An old friend (from childhood), a very intimate friend, a crony.
adj. (f. -ī), Lame, limping; — s.m. A lame man, a cripple.
wety: According to local folklore, named for a physically disabled لَن٘گْڑا (laṅgṛā, “crippled”) farmer, who first cultivated the variety.

def: adj. P. S. लंग لنگ Lame; cripple. 1. ' Kyā daulat andhī thī?' 'Huzūr? andhī na hotī to lūle langṛoṅ ke ghar kyoṅ ātī? ' 'Was Daulat (Fortune) then born blind?' 'Your Majesty! if she had not been blind would she have come to the house of a cripple?' (The answer was made (in Persian) by a blind woman named Daulat to Taimūr Lang, the Tamerlane of History). 2. Hāth pāoṅ ke lūle laṅgṛe, nām Salāmat Rāë! Prov. A cripple, and 'Sound' his name! langṛā'nā, laṅgṛā-kar chalnā , v. n. To limp; walk lame.
s.f. Limping, lameness.

ety: laṅgṛā + āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Limping, lameness.
s. f. Worthlessness.
s. f. Lameness.
adv. With a limp, limping, lame
subs: v.n. To walk lame, to limp.

ety: laṅgṛā, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव = आवे = S. आपि caus. increment; + nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To limp, to go (or walk) lame.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit लङ्गति (laṅgati) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀟- (-ḍa-). Related to لَن٘گْڑا (laṅgṛā, “crippled, limp”).

def: part. adj. Limping, walking lame

prep. = lagūṅ = lag, q.v.; — 2˚ s.m. = laguʼā, q.v. prep. = lag, q.v. s.f. Harmonious relation; affinity; correlation; bearing; appositeness, adaptedness (of time, or place, or of means with an end, or of appearances with a fact or truth, &c.); relevancy; consistency, concurrence, correspondence, reciprocal suitableness or agreeableness; (in Math.) ratio; — attachment, affection, love; — an application, or a direction (of the mind), aiming; aim; attention; exertion, endeavour, attempt; — touching, reaching, attaining (to), approach; cost, expenditure; — hitting, striking; fixing; — an attack of ill-fortune, a calamitous occurrence, a blow, stroke; enmity, animosity, hostility, rancour, spite, grudge; rivalry, competition; — narcotic quality (of a substance); — intrigue, plot; a secret; — trick, legerdemain, sleight of hand, jugglery; a charm, spell, fascination; — catch, hold, support, basis, ground; a prop

(-se), To be related (to), to belong (to), pertain (to); to relate (to), to have reference (to), to have to do (with); to have an interest (in); to be intent (on); — to harbour animosity or ill-feeling (against), to have a grudge (against)

s.f. Attachment, fondness; partiality, favouritism (syn. t̤araf-dārī); — reserve; — deceit
(-kī), To be enamoured (of), to fall in love (with)

adj. & adv. Without connection or tie, unconnected, disconnected, detached, separate; unfettered, free; — impartial, neutral; disinterested; unconcerned, indifferent; — unaided, unsupported; — without let or hindrance; without fail; — genuine, pure, unmixed; without enmity or spite; without any trickery; frank, candid, sincere; — without reserve; frankly; impartially, fairly, &c.

s.m. = lāṅgūl, q.v.

s.m. A heap, a large quantity, much; — s.f. Loin, reins
more: n. m. A heap; store; large quantity. Paisā hī paisā kar-ke laṅk lagtā hai. laṅk lagnā , v. n. To be piled; heaped.
s. f. The reins, the loins.
subs: (-kā), A heap, or pile (of things) to be formed; — to be heaped, or piled.

more: s. Name of the capital of Rāvaṇa in Ceylon.

vulg. laṅkes (˚kā+īś˚), s.m. 'Lord of laṅkā,' an epithet of any king of Laṅkā, but especially of Rāvaṇ.

(lit. thou shalt never see me) s. f. Boasting, airs, conceitedness.

s.f. Boasting, gasconading; airs, conceitedness. (The words are taken from the Qǒrʼān, Sūra vii. 149, al aʻrāf, 'And when Moses came at our appointed time, and his Lord spake unto him, he said, "O Lord, show thyself to me, that I may behold thee" (rabbi arinī, anz̤ur ilai-ka): He said, "Thou shalt not see Me (lan tarā-nī); but look towards the mount, and if it stand firm in its place, then shalt thou see Me" ')
more: n. f. lit . you cant see me. Boasting ( shekhī ).

s.m. A rope; the rope by which the ćaras or leathern bucket is drawn up from a well (syn. bart); cable (of a boat); — the quantity of land irrigated by one lāʼo in a day (in some places the quantity of land irrigated is reckoned by the lāʼo, instead of by wells, and is estimated at fifteen acres to a lāʼo); — a debt secured by a pledge
more: n. m. 1. The rope by which the leather bucket of a well is drawn up. In some places the amount of land irrigated is reckoned by the lāo , instead of wells, being commonly 15 acres to a lāo . 2. The quantity of land irrigated by one lāo in one day. 3. The cable of a boat. 4. Demand; request. 5. A debt secured by a pledge. lāo uṭhānā , v. n. To advance money to a cultivator for food, fodder, and seed. lāo charas , n. Irrigation by water raised from wells in the bucket called Charas . It is called purhāī when cattle are used, and garrā when manual labor is employed. Carnegy. lāo chalānā , v. n. To irrigate from a well. lāo lagānā , v. n. To assert a right to, and take over from a debtor property of any kind in satisfaction of a debt. lāo lagānā , n. m. Payment in kind.
s. m. 1. A rope. 2. The cable of a boat.
subs: v.n. To advance money to a cultivator for food fodder and seed
s.m. The rope and leathern bucket (for irrigation); irrigation by water raised from wells in the ćaras or leathern bucket
'To work the well-rope,' to irrigate from a well
(-par), To assert a right (to), and take over (from a debtor) property (of any kind) in satisfaction of a debt; — s.m. Payment in kind.

s. f. A handful, the space in the palm of the hand closed so as to hold water.

def: s.f. Spring, bound; bounce; a flash (of lightning, &c.), dart; a snatch; — beat, throb, pulsation, palpitation; — springiness, elasticity, flexibility; nimbleness, dexterity, swiftness; — a bad habit
more: n. f. 1. A flash (of lightning, etc.). 2. A spring; bound. 3. Beat; pulsation; throb; palpitation. 4. ( lapak jhapak ) Swiftness; nimbleness; dexterity. 5. Elasticity, flexibility. 6. A bad habit. lapak jhapak se , adv. Swiftly; dexterously.
(from لپکنا) s. f. 1. A flash (of lightning, &c.). 2. The spring (of a tiger, &c.), bounce, snatch.
subs: adv. With a spring or bound; with a hop, skip, and a jump; nimbly, briskly, swiftly, dexterously.

ety: lapak˚ = Prk. लप्पक्क(इ) or लप्पक्के(इ), prob. fr. S. लम्फ+कृ
def: v.n. To spring (upon, — par), to dart (at), to snatch (at); to make an attack (on); to bound, bounce; to catch (a ball, &c.); to flash, dart (as lightning); to beat, throb, palpitate; to rush (after), to give chase (to); to hurry, make haste
more: v. a. 1. E. To flash (as lightning). Bijlī lapaktī hai . 2. To beat; pulsate; palpitate; throb. Is vaqt nabz khūb lapak rahī hai . 3. To spring; bound ( uchhalnā , 1). 4. To make haste; run; give chase ( dauṛnā , 1); to put one's best leg foremost. 5. To spring upon; attack. 6. To catch (a ball). 7. To seize; grasp; snatch; gripe; clutch. Upar kī ūpar hī lapak lī . 8. To snap up; snap at; hook; claw.
v. n. To flash (as, lightning), to spring, bounce or rush forth or upon, to attack.
subs: v.t. To spring forward and seize; to seize, snatch; to snap at or up; to hook, claw.

def: adj. Slender, thin, slight, delicate.
n. f. 1. Jargon; jumble. 2. Perversion of the truth; prevarication; equivocation; suggestio falsī; suppressio veri. lapaṛ shapaṛ , adv. Awkwardly.

careless, uncaring

def: adj. 1. Wrapped up; folded. 2. Twined; twisted; coiled. 3. Ornamented with gold or silver thread wound round it. 4. Hidden; secret; latent. Lapeṭvāṅ gālī . 5. Roundabout; indirect. Lapeṭvāṅ bāt. lapeṭvāṅ , adv. Indirectly; by way of inuendo.
v.n. To jump, leap, spring.

adj. & adv. 'Going by springs and bounds'; rapid and bouncing (as a gait); active, nimble, dapper, quick; — nimbly, briskly, quickly, smartly; — s.f. Nimbleness, briskness, dexterity; — pouncing upon and carrying off, pilfering, stealing.

adj. Nimble; quick; fast. Bāp chup chup, pūt lap-jhap . Prov. The father so quiet, the son so sharp. lap jhap , adv. Quickly; smartly; sharp; in a trice; nimbly. Rāṅdhūṅ, rī dhī merī, motī chhaṛā bhāt, harī harī mūṅg disāorī, Phulke to, merī dhī, lap jhap poūṅ, ghī bhar ḍālūṅ ṭoknā! Song. Marvan. I'll cook rice pearly white, Daughter! with green dāl bright and rare, The cakes I'll briskly bake, Daughter! nor keg of butter spare. lap-jhap , n. f. 1. Dexterity; nimbleness; swiftness. Yeh aurat apnī lap jhap se har ek chīz churā letī hai . 2. Stealing; pilfering; cribbing. Muglāniyāṅ kabhī apnī lap jhap se bāz nahīṅ ātīṅ. lap-jhap chāl , n. f. A rapid awkward gait. Aisī lap jhap chāl chaltā hai ki ek na ek chīz gir paṛtī hai . Wom. lupar lupar h., lup lup k., luplupānā , Slang, v. n. To beat or throb (the heart with fear).
s.m. A bound forward (in order to snatch); a snatch; — nimbleness, &c. ( = lapak); — a bad habit; a vitiated taste; — one who snatches anything from another
more: n. m. 1. A bad habit. Laṛātā hai muā jis tis se āṅkheṅ, Use be-tarah yeh lapkā paṛā hai . Wom. Rangin. 2. A vitiated taste. 3. ( Brij .) One who snatches anything from another. 4. A loose character; a vagabond. lapkā paṛnā , v. n. To be addicted to a bad habit. Use to harām ke māl kā lapkā paṛ gayā hai .

s. m. 1. A snatch. 2. Nimbleness. 3. A bad custom or habit. Mīr says: اپني توجـﮩان آنکهـ لڙي پهر وهين ديکهو آينه کو لپکا هي پريشان نظري کا Upnī to jahāṅ ānkh laṛī phir wahīṅ dekho; āʼīne ko lapkā hai pareshān-naz̤arī kā. Where I first fell in love, there you will see me; (I am not like) the mirror (which) is in the habit of casting its glances in all directions.

n. f. See rāṛ . 1. Contention; quarrel; brawl. 1. Jorū khasam kī laṛāī, dūdh kī sī malāī . Prov. Wives and husbands quarrel away, Cream will rise skim as you may. 2. Khālī ādmī kahā kare, ghar meṅ baiṭh laṛāī laṛe? Prov. A lone man in the house, what can he do but quarrel and fight? 2. Conflict; contest; skirmish; affray; warfare. Laṛāī bhiṛāī par khāk ḍālo, apne dam kī khair māṅgo! Your quarrels bury, a blessing crave! 3. Wrestling; boxing; pugilism; fight; fighting; combat. 4. Battle; war; campaign. 5. Hostility; enmity. Sārī paise kī laṛāī hai . Money is the root of quarrels. laṛāī bāṅdhnā , v. a. To make a quarrel. laṛāī baṛhānā , v. a. See rāṛ baṛhānā. laṛāī palle bāṅdhnā, yā mol lenā , v. a. lit . to buy a quarrel. To involve oneself in a fray or quarrel. 1. Udhār denā, laṛāī mol lenā . Prov. Give a loan, a quarrel own. 2. Apnā do, aur laṛāī mol lo! Prov. Give of your own, a quarrel buy! (Lend your money and lose your friend). (A. Al-qarz miqrāz-ul-mohabbat ). 3. Sach kahnā ādhī laṛāī mol lenā hai . Prov. To speak the truth is to purchase half a quarrel. laṛāī pe jānā, yā chaṛhnā , v. a. To go to war. laṛāī tulnā . To be imminent (a war). wety: Inherited from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *लडिक्क (laḍikka). Related to the lexicographically-attested Sanskrit लाडिक (lāḍika, “boy, servant”), लट्व (laṭva, “dancing boy”) लट (laṭa, “fool”), and thus may ultimately come from Sanskrit लट् (laṭ, “to act like a child”) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓𑁆𑀓- (-kka-). Cognate to Awadhi लरिका (larikā), Bhojpuri लईका (laīkā), Bengali লেড়কা (leṛoka).

def: n. m. S. लड् to play, or S. लट् to be a child. See bachchā (2, 3). A boy; son. 1. Laṛkoṅ meṅ laṛkā, būṛhoṅ meṅ būṛhā . Prov. Saying. With children a child, with old men an old man. 2. Binā chūchī bārah baras laṛkā rakkhe . Prov. The child for twelve years kept without the breast. (To delude with false hopes). 3. Bāīṅ bagal mero laṛkā sove, ochhī khāṭ bipat moko, Sarkat āve, ṭhaur kahāṅ to ko ? Rus. Wom. song. 4. Ham to janaliau hahīṅ ge laṛikvā? torā pāle baṛ baṛ bheḍ, Kiryā khailā se ham nahīṅ manbau, debau nai-harvā raged! Maggah Song. I took you for a child, great art beneath your skirt you hide, Your oaths I'll not believe, back to your father's house you'll stride. 5. Laṛkoṅ kā khel, chiṛyoṅ kā maran . Prov. What's sport for the boys is death to the birds. 6. Laṛke ko jab bheṛyā le gayā, tab ṭaṭṭī bāṅdhī . Prov. After the wolf has carried off the child a matted door is put on. (When the horse was stolen the stable door was shut). 7. Bagal meṅ laṛkā, shahr meṅ ḍhaṅḍhorā . Prov. The child in arms, the crier cries 'Lost child.' laṛkā , adj. See bachchah. laṛkā bālā , n. m. Children ( bachchah ). Kuchh laṛkā-bālā hone-vālā hai . She expects (a child.) laṛkā jannā , v. n. To give birth to a son; to be delivered of a son. Laṛkā jane bībī, aur paṭṭī bāṅdhe miyāṅ! Prov. The wife delivered of a child, the bandage round the husband's head! (One bears the pains, another has the gains). laṛkā god lenā , v. a. To adopt (a son). laṛkoṅ kā khel . See bachchoṅ kā khel. laṛke-bāloṅ kī khabar na lenā . To neglect wife and children. laṛke-bāle . Family; household. laṛke-vālā , n. m. laṛke-vālī , n. f. The father of the bridegroom. rāh bāṭ kā laṛkā . A foundling.

Aur woh jo āshnā se jhagṛī haiṅ, yā laṛī haiṅ, Muṅh ko chhupā palaṅg pe machlī huī paṛī haiṅ . Nazīr. And those who with their lovers have a quarrel had In sullen mood their faces hide and lie in bed. 2. Do laṛte haiṅ to ek girtā hī hai . Prov. When two fight one must fall. 4. To go to war; wage war; give battle. Laṛe na bhiṛe, zirah pahne phire! Prov. No stomach for a fight, in armour struts a knight! 5. To hit; strike against; collide with ( bhiṛnā , 2). 6. E. To bite; sting. Tataiyā laṛ gayā . 7. To agree or coincide with. Hisāb laṛnā . 8. To be staked, risked. Jān laṛ rahī hai . 9. To subscribe; contribute one's quota. ety: las˚ = Prk. लस(इ) = S. लस(ति), rt. लस्
def: v.n. To shine, to look bright or beautiful; — to be becoming (to), to become, befit; — to be or become viscous, or glutinous, or sticky; to stick (to); to fit tight or well (as a cap, &c.); to be tenacious or stiff (as soil); to be moist; to be smeared or plastered (with)
more: v. n. S. लस to shine. To shine; look bright or beautiful. Sakhī, sohat Gopāl ke ur gunjan kī māl, Bāhar lasat hiye māno dāvānal kī juāl . Satsaī. My dear, the necklace red Gopal's neck doth adorn As breast-consuming flames which all the forest burn. (Rādha describes the jealousy which burned in her own breast on seeing Krishna with a necklace made by her rival).
v. n. S. लष To be sticky, viscous, glutinous.
v. n. 1. To be smeared, plastered. 2. To stick to; fit like a cap. Yeh phabtī us par lis gaī .

v. n. 1. To become, to befit. 2. To shine. Bihārī says:-- سکهي سوهت گوپال کي ار گنجن کي مال باهرلست هيي منودابانل کي جوال Sakhī sohat gopāl ke ur gunjan kī māl; bāhar lasat hiye mano dābānal kī jwāl. Oh my friend! that necklace of the seeds of Abrus adorns the breast of Gopāl; it appears opposite to his heart like the flame of a fire which consumes the forest. (A speech of Rādhā in the Satsaʼī on seeing Kr̤ish̤ṇa wear such a necklace, the work of her rival. She compares it to fire, both from the colour, and the effect which the sight of it has on her bosom).
subs: v.n. intens. of and = lasnā (the form lisnā appears to differ from lasnā in being used to express the idea of 'viscosity,' &c. only; cf. lisauṛā).

Kyoṅ bahisht meṅ lāt mārte ho? Prov. Why do you kick away Heaven from yourself? (You only hurt yourself). 2. Bachche te khilāṅde dūd to bhāt, bāre hūne te mār de lāt . Panj. Prov. On milk and rice an infant raise, grown up with kicks you he repays. 3. Tīn din ke chhokrā hameṅ sikhāvat bāt! Jable woh liheṅ ṭhikṛā, table mārib lāt . Bhoj. Prov. 'Dekhat hī chaṭkāve lāteṅ, Jhūū bhar bhus rāë ko khāë, gobar karan hār ko jāë, Bāje seṅṭh, na bāje rāī, māṅg khāt the so ū gai .' A satire on a…

A sudden fit or emotion. Baiṭhe baiṭhe yeh kyā laṭak sūjhī! 5. The influence of any spirit. Saiyad kī laṭak. laṭak chāl , n. f. 1. Reeling steps. 2. Wanton gait.

more: (from لٿکنا) s. f. 1. Hanging, dangling. 2. An affected motion in blandishment and coquetry. لٿک چال laṭak-chāl, s. f. Affected gait, coquetry.

def: v.n. To hang, to depend; to hang loosely, to dangle, to swing; to be suspended, to be swung; — to be kept waiting or in suspense; to be put off, be postponed
more: v. n. To hang, to dangle.
subs: or laṭak-jānā, v.n. To hang, dangle; to be or become suspended; to hang ooneself (on or to, -meṅ)
v.n. To remain or continue hanging (on or over, -meṅ); to linger (over), to loiter, tarry, delay.
wety: Urdu verb set لَٹَکْنَا (laṭaknā) لَٹْکانا (laṭkānā) Inherited from Prakrit *𑀮𑀝𑁆𑀝 (*laṭṭa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓𑁆𑀓- (-kka-). Cognate with Gujarati લટકવું (laṭakvũ), Marathi लटकणे (laṭakṇe).

def: v. n. 1. To hang; dangle; to be suspended, slung. 2. To hang oneself. Peṛ meṅ rassī ḍāl-ke laṭak gayā . 3. To loiter; tarry; delay. Baras din se Gulistāṅ hī meṅ laṭak rahā hai . 4. To be postponed, deferred, put off. 5. To be kept waiting ( jhūlnā . 2).
n. f. Early cold weather crops.
s.f. An early-sown crop, a crop sown during the cold season (and hence 'a poor or impoverished crop').
s.f. An old shoe ( = latrī).
n. f. 1. Overwork. 2. Trial (اَفت 2). Baṛī latāṛ meṅ phaṅse huë haiṅ . 3. Excess; shower; crush. In dinoṅ meṅ baṛī kām kī latāṛ hai . 4. Fatigue; the sweat of one's brow; exhaustion.

s.f. Contumelious treatment, spurning, reviling (syn. apavād); — labour, toil, exertion, overwork, fatigue, exhaustion (syn. glāni, śram); pressure, press, crush, excess (of); — calamity, misfortune, trial.
lottery
v. a. 1. See āṛe hāth lenā , (2-5). 2. To overwork; to fatigue; wear out. Yeh kapṛoṅ ko khūb latāṛtā hai .

v.t. To treat with contumely or disdain, to spurn, to revile, to insult, to affront; to bring down; — to work, to overwork, to make (one) toil or use exertion, to exhaust by labour, to fatigue, jade, wear out.
more: v. a. To work, to cause to toil, exhaust by labour, fatigue, jade, to bring down, to affront, to insult.

def: n. m. S. यष्टि or लष्टि 1. A club; cudgel. 2. A measuring rod or pole. In the N. W. P. the measuring chain ( jarīb ) is divided into 10 laṭṭhās or gaṭṭhas , and each laṭṭhā into 10 karis links. A bīghā is a square of 20 laṭṭhās. laṭh-bāzī , n. Club-fighting. laṭh chalnā , v. n. To fight with clubs. laṭh mārnā , v. a. To speak harshly. dhūl ke laṭh lagānā , v. n. To tell a lie. s. m. A stick, club, cudgel. (See لاٿهـ). لٿهـ بازي laṭh-bāz-ī, s. f. Cudgelling.

Church in a Mufassil station. lāṭh sāhab kā daftar, lāṭh sāhab ke daftar kā dār-ul-inshā , n. The Secretariat. lāṭh sāhab ke tan ke mahāfiz . The Bodyguard. lāṭh musāhib , n. m. n. f. S. लगुड़ or यष्टि Pr. laṭṭhī . 1. A staff. 2. A pillar; column; an obelisk; a minar. 3. The upright beam which revolves in a mill. Ek kolhū bārah lāṭh, charkhā chale tin sau sāṭh . One mill with columns twelve, and revolutions three hundred and sixty. (The twelve signs of the zodiac and 360 days). 4. A ḍheṅklī , 1. q. v. for drawing water. 5. The pestle which works in the mortar or press in which the juice is expressed.

s.m. A large club or staff; a pillar, &c. ( = lāṭh, q.v.). s.f. def: n. f. S. यष्टि Pr. laṭṭhī . A club; cudgel; bludgeon. 1. Hazār lāṭhī ṭūṭī ho, ghar bār ke bāsan toṛne ko bahut hai . Prov. A thousand times the stick may broken be, to break the pots and pans 'twill do. (When the husband is in a rage he breaks the pots and pans to spite his wife who has to go without her dinner). 2. Ek lāṭhī sab ko hāṅktā hai . Prov. He drives every one with the same stick. (He does not pay each one the deference due to his rank and position). lāṭhī pāṭhī , n.
